template_name: EU General-Purpose AI Code of Practice

provisions.normative_text_ref: GPAI Code of Practice Transparency Commitment 1
provisions.description: GPAI model providers shall publish a sufficiently detailed training data summary including modalities, data size, language and demographic coverage, and intended purpose, as required by Art. 53(1)(d) of the EU AI Act

sub_provisions.description: The training data summary must cover data modalities, size, and language coverage used for model pre-training
Demographic and representativeness characteristics of training data must be documented

evaluation.message: At least one data_provenance record is required — GPAI Code of Practice Transparency Commitment 1 mandates a sufficiently detailed training data summary
Every data_provenance record must specify source_type (licensed, scraped, public_domain, synthetic, user_generated) per the GPAI training data summary requirements

At least one data_provenance record with summary_type='training_data_summary' is required — the GPAI Code of Practice mandates a structured training data summary with modalities, size, and coverage details
Data provenance records should specify data modalities (text, image, audio, video, code, etc.) per GPAI training data summary disclosure requirements
Data provenance records should be linked to a subject entity to identify which GPAI model the training data summary pertains to

provisions.normative_text_ref: GPAI Code of Practice Transparency Commitment 2
provisions.description: GPAI model providers shall document individual data sources including source URIs, acquisition methods, crawler identifiers, and crawl timing windows for web-scraped data

sub_provisions.description: For web-scraped data, documentation must include top domains, crawler identifiers, and crawl timing windows

evaluation.message: At least one data_provenance record is required — GPAI Code of Practice Transparency Commitment 2 mandates documentation of individual data sources
Every data_provenance record must specify source_uris — the GPAI Code of Practice requires documented data source locations for transparency
Data provenance records should specify the acquisition_method (licensed, scraped, public_domain, synthetic) for each data source per GPAI transparency requirements
Data provenance records should be linked to dataset entities for proper data source traceability

provisions.normative_text_ref: GPAI Code of Practice Copyright Commitment 1
provisions.description: GPAI model providers shall comply with text and data mining (TDM) opt-out mechanisms per Directive (EU) 2019/790 Art. 4, including detection and respect of robots.txt, TDMRep protocol, and HTTP headers indicating rights reservations

sub_provisions.description: Providers must implement opt-out detection methods including robots.txt, TDMRep, and HTTP headers
Providers must document their process for removing or excluding opted-out content from training data

evaluation.message: At least one copyright_rights_reservation record is required — GPAI Code of Practice Copyright Commitment 1 mandates documented TDM opt-out compliance per EU Directive 2019/790 Art. 4
Every copyright_rights_reservation record must specify the opt_out_detection_method (robots.txt, TDMRep, HTTP headers) used for TDM rights reservation compliance
Copyright rights reservation records should include a compliance_verification_date documenting when TDM opt-out compliance was last verified
Copyright rights reservation records should be linked to a subject entity to identify which GPAI model's training data the TDM compliance applies to

provisions.normative_text_ref: GPAI Code of Practice Copyright Commitment 2
provisions.description: GPAI model providers shall document licensing agreements for copyrighted content used in training, including licensor, scope, content type, and agreement references

evaluation.message: At least one license_record is required — GPAI Code of Practice Copyright Commitment 2 mandates documentation of licensing agreements for copyrighted training content
License records should specify the licensor to establish the granting party for training data licensing compliance
License records should specify the scope of the licensing agreement to verify coverage of intended training uses

provisions.normative_text_ref: GPAI Code of Practice Safety Commitment 1
provisions.description: Providers of GPAI models with systemic risk shall maintain a safety and security framework document covering responsibilities, evaluation triggers, risk forecasting methodology, and mitigations

sub_provisions.description: The safety framework must define responsibilities, evaluation triggers, and risk forecasting methodology
The safety framework must document mitigations for identified systemic risks

evaluation.message: At least one governance_policy record is required — GPAI Code of Practice Safety Commitment 1 mandates a safety and security framework document for systemic risk GPAI models
Safety framework governance_policy records should include attached framework documents (PDF or similar) demonstrating comprehensive safety and security coverage
Safety framework governance_policy records should be linked to a subject entity to identify which systemic risk GPAI model the framework covers

provisions.normative_text_ref: GPAI Code of Practice Safety Commitment 2
provisions.description: Providers of GPAI models with systemic risk shall conduct and document systemic risk assessments covering CBRN risks, loss of control, cyber offense, manipulation, and other categories, including adversarial and red-team evaluation results

sub_provisions.description: Risk assessments must cover CBRN, loss of control, cyber offense, and manipulation risk categories
Risk assessments must include adversarial and red-team evaluation results with independent evaluator identification

evaluation.message: At least one risk_register record is required — GPAI Code of Practice Safety Commitment 2 mandates documented systemic risk assessments for GPAI models classified as having systemic risk
Every risk_register record must specify risk_category (e.g., cbrn, loss_of_control, cyber_offense, manipulation) per GPAI systemic risk assessment requirements
Risk register records should specify the assessment_method (adversarial testing, red-team evaluation, expert review) used for systemic risk assessment
Risk register records should be linked to a subject entity to identify which systemic risk GPAI model was assessed

provisions.normative_text_ref: GPAI Code of Practice Safety Commitment 3
provisions.description: Providers of GPAI models with systemic risk shall report serious incidents to the AI Office within 2-15 days of becoming aware, documenting incident type, severity, notification date, and AI Office reference

sub_provisions.description: Incident reports must be submitted to the AI Office within 2 days for immediate notification and 15 days for the full report

evaluation.message: At least one incident_report record is required — GPAI Code of Practice Safety Commitment 3 mandates documented incident reporting to the AI Office for systemic risk GPAI models
Every incident_report record must specify severity to determine the notification timeline (2-day vs 15-day) per GPAI incident reporting requirements
Incident reports should include notification_date to verify compliance with the 2-15 day reporting timeline to the AI Office
Incident reports should be linked to a subject entity to identify which GPAI model experienced the incident
